tokens: Hey, Bob, how do you read?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: We're parked on a heading of 107.
(Jack is laughing) Are you happy with that?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Laughing) You parked on a slope, too.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: There's no level...There's no level spot to park, here, though.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: You want some help getting off?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I just about ended up down at the bottom of the hill.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay; (static) 192, 3.8, 3.1; 88 and 80; (static ends) 108 and 0 on the batteries.
The forward motors are 220 and about 270, and the rears are off-scale low and 220.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: You want me to block the wheels?
(Both laugh) You got the brake on, I hope.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Pause) I don't know if I can lean uphill enough (to get off)!
Boy, are we on a slope!
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Let me get this thing set again.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I don't think you can get a (gravimeter reading)...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Boy, are we on a slope!
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I'm going to stay out from between the rocks.
It's a beautiful east-west split rock.
It's even got a north overhang that we can work with.
(Pause) And let me see what it (the boulder) is!
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And this boulder's got its own little track!
Right up the hill, cross contour.
It's a chain-of-craters track, and it looks like it stops (static) off where it started.
It starts in, what looks to be, a lighter-colored linear zone.
Trying to give you perspective, it's probably only about a third of the way up the North Massif.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Read you loud and clear; and we've got a picture.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Oh, man, I tell you, are we parked on a slope!
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: It's a coarsely vesicular, crystalline rock...finely crystalline.
Looks like, probably, an anorthositic gabbro.
(I'm) trying to see the zap pits for glass color; I don't have a good one yet.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Say, Bob, you want both the (SEP) recorder and the other switch off?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Both of those off, and dusted.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Oh, man, is it hard to get around here.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Bob, it looks like the glass is fairly light colored.
It's anorthositic gabbro, rather than gabbroic anorthosite (in composition), I think.
Yeah, that's black glass in the pits.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And, Gene, did you happen to notice the temperature on the SEP when you dusted it?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Bob, some of the vesicles are...They're flattened.
There's a strong foliation of vesicles in the rock.
Most of them are flattened, and they are up to 15 or 20 centimeters in diameter and about 5 to 6 centimeters thick ...or wide.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And there's some beautiful north overhangs all around the block.
Well, (laughs) on the north side of the block.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: That's the best place to have north overhang; and I guess that means one of you guys might grab the SEC - the small can - before you leave the Rover.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: It's going to take me a while to dust.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: You want the north overhang sample in the SEC - or the short can?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: They don't want the short can.
I'm not sure I understand that, Jack, but they don't want the short can here, they say.
(Pause) I guess they're looking for volcanics today (at Station 9).
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Okay, we'll put them in bags.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: They're looking for volcanics today, Jack.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Well, they're hoping (to find volcanic material) again at Station 9 (and want to save the sealed "short can" for that possibility).
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Now, that foliation I mentioned does not go all the way through the rock.
There's another; it almost looks like a large - it is - a large inclusion of non-vesicular rock within the vesicular rock.
There may be some auto-brecciation involved in the formation of this thing.
It really looks, mineralogically, like the light-colored (breccia) samples from the South Massif.
But I tell you, that's only because it's light colored, and I...I can't give you anymore than that right now, until we get a fresh surface.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: 110 degrees on the SEP and you want the cover closed, right?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And did you get the batteries,...
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: No, I didn't copy that you wanted them open.
I was about to ask you that.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And, Jack, while I'm interrupting everybody here,...
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: ...how about a frame count, if convenient.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: I gave you one at the (Turning Point) rock.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Man, I never...(Laughing) You can't believe how tough it is getting around this Rover, on this slope!!
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Man, that...I think we're probably pitched 20 and rolled 20!
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I think I'll get over here and get a pan while we're waiting to sample.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Oh, I got to dust those radiators.
I can't leave them like that.
I tell you, this is not a very good place to dust them, though.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I need a little finesse, though.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: It's one thing to reach over here and do this on level ground.
(Pause) I don't know if I can do that without falling on the battery.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Well, I found a place to stand where I can take a pan.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Bob, I'm going to have to give you a good battery brushing at the next site.
I can't get...I can get half of them, but I can't get the other half.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: What are you working on, Jack?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (To Bob) I bet you a dollar to doughnuts that you don't get a
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Gene, if it's easy enough to take it off, why don't you take it off the Rover; and we'll try and level it in the stuff (the soil).
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (Responding to Bob's suggestion about putting the
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I'm not sure there's any place to put it on the ground level.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: No, you have to dig a place.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Not convinced this is a good idea) Yeah, I'll do it.
Well, I'll set it right up here.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: It's going to fall down the hill.
You'd better stomp off a good place.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (To Jack) Can you see it from there?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay; now let me get to work.
Straighten out my fender (which) got a little kinked here, which isn't going to help us.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And we see your gold visor is up.
You may want to put it down out here in the Sun.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Well, I think I might...I can't see with it down; it's scratched!
I think I can monitor that one (meaning his visor).
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Hey, I'm standing on a boulder track.
(To Gene) How does that make you feel?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: That makes me feel like I'm coming over to do some sampling.
(Pause) Think how it would have been if you were standing there before that boulder came by.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I'd rather not think about it.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: You got a spot picked while you're here?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Well, the big thing is, let's get the boulder and then get in that east-west split.
Bob, I got an undocumented sample from the middle of the boulder track.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Pause) Gene, if you hit them off in there, it's going to be awful hard to find them, that's the problem.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Did you pick a spot - a good spot - while you were over here?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I just was looking at it.
I think we need to get in the light, though.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I can see with my gold visor up.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Let me put a sample in your bag (SCB).
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: This boulder looks fairly uniform from top to bottom.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: We've got to get a reference sample of this soil.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Let's get where we can get that 90-degree picture, too.
So we really ought to get on the Sun side.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Pointing at a spot about shoulder height on the boulder) Let me get that slab right there, though, to start with.
I can get that one off.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Well, there's no...Let's go over on the Sun side because we can't really photograph it.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I got to get out of here first.
Let's go through the split (between the boulders).
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Pause) Why don't we sample the split first so we don't...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Man, I tell you, if you get your shovel down there, you'd have a (garbled).
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Yes, let's sample in the split first so that we don't get it too messed up.
And then we can sample some of this stuff.
Not...(Gesturing south) We want this overhang over here, Geno - the north facing one.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I got to sneak by over there.
Don't shuffle too much dirt in there.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: You by me so I can set the gnomon down.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Don't think I can make it - without hitting you.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Let me set the gnomon down...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Set it down just outside the shadow there.
There's still some good clean ground there.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I can get back far enough to take these pictures.
I want to go get a stereo pan around the corner anyway.
Let's see if I can't start here with about (f-stop) 5.6.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I must have a boulder behind me.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I'm going to go around the cor(ner)...I got it now.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I'm going to get the shadowed material.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Holding the sample bag) It's in bag 312, Bob.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And it's...It's from...I think you saw where I got it.
It's about a half a meter back of the limit of the overhang.
(To Gene) Put it (the sample bag) down.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: You can turn it a little bit towards me.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Pause; presenting his SCB) And the soil outside the overhang will be next.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And the first one is from the upper 2 centimeters.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Pouring) And the second one is from probably 2 centimeters down to about 8 (centimeters).
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Bob, it looks like the fragment - or the boulder - just to the south of us has some inclusions in it - light-colored inclusions.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: You mean the south half of the split boulder?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: I haven't seen inclusions in the other half.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Yeah, we're happy with that for an east/west split.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (No longer hammering) I've got to find a corner I can get at.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Moving north, away from the boulder) Let me get an "after" picture down in this hole (that is, where they got the soil sample).
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: You almost stepped on the...I forgot the "after", too.
(Pause) Hey, there are chips up here on top, also.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: We can get some of those, but...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Looks like somebody's been chipping up there.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Looks like there's been a geologist here before us.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I think I can get some of these pieces over here.
I want to get that 90-degree angular flight line around this boulder, too.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Bob, the more I look at this thing...Now, here's the piece that fell off.
Here's the piece that was knocked off up there.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: We ought to bring a big piece of that home.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: How about this one up here?
I think we can just lift that off.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I was going to get my down-Sun, but I'm afraid I'll...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: You may be down-Sun if you do.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Just throw it in my bag.
That's a nice, big piece, too.
It's about the size of a...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Laughing) Why don't you put it in mine.
I can't get up to you.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Backing up to take a series of flightline photos) Don't move.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, Bob, there's a big spall lying on the ground here that has been knocked off up there, from right on top of the boulder.
And, I tell you, the more I look at the south half of this boulder, the more heterogeneous in texture it looks.
It looks as if it may be either a re-crystallized breccia of some kind, or you had a gabbroic anorthosite magma catch up an awful lot of inclusions.
I guess I prefer the latter explanation (pause) because of the extreme vesicularity of the rock.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Now, a few of the inclusions are.
Well, they're all subrounded to rounded, and a few of them are very light colored.
I'm going to try to get...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I'm coming around the corner with a flightline stereo.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Are you going to do it now?
Well, you know, I ought to get one shot back here with a black and white.
I'll get this half black and white.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I think we ought to pick up a piece of that spall there by the gnomon...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Thinking about getting a piece off the top of to south fragments) I can break it off.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: There's one right by the gnomon we can just pick up.
It's a finer-grained vesicular rock than...Wait...Where...Jeez...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I thought I was going to get this half.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Well, they like to have some of it in black and white, you know.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I forgot to look at the objectives for the station.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Well, we want to get 500's of that boulder track.
I know I want to get that.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: A piece of that spalled rock that was sitting by the gnomon...Ooh, watch out gnomon.
How about that?...(The sample) is in bag 535.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: You got one in there already?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (To Jack, who is trying to get into position to put the sample bag in Gene's SCB) You won't be able to reach...You won't be able to reach my bag.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: No, but you can put it in mine.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Bob, one of the light-colored inclusions looks like it may be anorthositic...(Correcting himself) gabbroic anorthosite...Let me get my terms straight.
The host rock has dark-enough zap pits that it's probably gab - ah - anorthositic gabbro, if I didn't say that.
Some of the light-colored inclusions have slightly lighter-colored glass, and they may be the gabbroic anorthosite.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (To Gene) Inclusions like this one and that one.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Some of those inclusions get to be bigger than the size of a baseball.
There's one here and a couple up there.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: See that one right on the ...Right there.
Right...(Pause) Well, that's a hard rock.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: You might be able to do it; I can't.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Pause) Okay, we need some of the soil outside the shadow here.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: How about over where your bag went?
Let's move around here...I think there is some...Oops!
Get on this slope over here.
Are we supposed to get a...(Glancing at
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I'd like to get...(Pause) Well, when you face uphill, your camera faces down.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: We want to get a rake on the rim of that little crater down there, I guess.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (To Gene, speaking under Bob) Let's get working.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: ...What we said this morning in terms of combining Stations 6 and 7 to an hour and 20 minutes...
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: And so it's sort of your option as to how much time you spend here and how much you go on to Station 7 and spend.
If you feel that it's worthwhile, we could spend essentially all that hour and 20 minutes at this station.
But if we did that, we'd like to get a fair variety of blocks, if they're available.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (To Gene) Geno, we can sample some of the light-colored group...As a matter of fact, this block looks different.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Well, so does that big one...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: ...it's a big blue-gray rock, itself is crystalline, I believe.
The inclusions are much more sharply defined, and it's non-vesicular; and it's included, or at least it's in contact with the very vesicular anorthositic gabbro.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Did you get some pictures of it?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: As I bounced around there, I took pictures of it.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Look, we can get some of that light-colored stuff in there, along with the blue-gray.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: We ought to get as big a piece of that inclusion as we can.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Pause) I think we're out of line-of-sight with them.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: We can hear you loud and clear.
We're just looking at rocks right now.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Okay, Bob, the boulder downslope (that is, Fragment 2) is more of a light-gray, vesicular boulder.
The one Jack just talked about (that is, Fragment 1) with some of the larger white inclusions is less vesicular, and it's more of blue-gray rock.
And if I don't fall on my tail here, I'll get...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, let me try and get up there.
We must be high enough to see something.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Let me get a close-up before you start pounding.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I might go from this angle too.
(Pause) A little different up in there too, Jack.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: We ought to try and sample that.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Let's get the...You want me to get my scoop under there?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Let's) get as many of these pieces as we can.
I don't know how many are going to come out.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Laughing) This whole thing will come out here in a minute.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Move your arm up or down.
I got it in case we don't get another one.
(Long Pause) Hey, we're getting good at that.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Let me get up on this...Up here.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Why don't we get a bag out.
Let me put these in a bag.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: That's why I'm getting up here so I can...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Bob, 556 is one of the light-colored inclusions in the blue-gray rock.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I think we lost that other one.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I got it; I know where it is.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: It's not a lot of sample, but it's representative, I think.
It looks a lot like that sugary rock I sampled yesterday (at Station 2), doesn't it?
Found in the...That we sampled in the...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Yeah, it's pretty easy to break up; it's really not very coherent (means "cohesive") at all.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: You know, I thought last night, Bob, that I should use the word "aplitic" for a texture that we saw in that inclusion yesterday on the South Massif.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: If I could keep from falling on my tail.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Pause) Okay, you going to get some of that?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Yeah, that's a different kind; that's a more beat up inclusion of some sort.
(Pause) Oh, there's a nice piece coming out.
Oh, wait a minute...Don't lose it.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: We have another inclusion that, on the surface, has a more reddish-brown texture (means "color").
Interior looks pretty much the same; it's a very light gray.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: This looks like a piece of breccia.
Looks like a fragment breccia that got caught up in this thing.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Yeah, well, the whole thing is obviously a breccia.
I'd sure like to get that...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Well, I'd say...I'm not sure (that) it's obviously a breccia.
I think it may be an igneous rock with breccia inclusions.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Well, okay, but look at all these things...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Which is sort of in the same class.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I can't get in there, Geno, you'll have to.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Trying to fit a sample in Gene's SCB) No way...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Pause) I think it's easier for you (to put the sample bag in Jack's SCB).
Did I give them a number on that?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Squash it...Cramp it a little bit, if you can; a little more.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Let's go get the host rock here.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: How about this one, with the inclusion?
Maybe I can get this one.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: That may have been a little optimistic.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Do you guys have a feeling that the two halves of the big boulder are different rocks?
Or is it the same rock split?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: No, they were all one boulder, I think.
They are just two major rock types in wherever they came from.
And I tried to describe that to you.
We have the contact in the central boulder (Fragment 2).
There're really three big boulders (Fragments 1, 2, and 4+5).
The central boulder had the contact between the light-gray rock - or the blue-gray rock - and the vesicular anorthositic gabbro.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And you guys have that pretty well photo-documented, right?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Yeah, it's in pretty good shape.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Try going on the side there, Geno.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Just went from the side, Jack.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: You got a piece of the...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I think you can get this one up here, can't you?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I wanted that one 'cause it had that inclusion wrapped in it.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Which one are you talking about?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Yeah, I just...(Pause) It's about to come.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I need this in a bag.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: They're both host rocks; we can put them in the same bag.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: ...is a chip of the blue-gray rock; and the blue-gray host rock...(Pause) And let me get that other one.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Pause) Pick the rock up while you're there.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Let me put) my little hammer somewhere.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And 538 is another sample of that material...a little dustier (sample).
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: That's the blue-gray, Bob, with the inclusions in it.
Now the blue-gray, the more you looked at it, it looks like a...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Give me your left...(correcting himself) Your right hand.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: How do you want it over?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: You kept turning it over in the same direction.
Like that, so I can fix that.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Now give me your bag, and I'll get it in there.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (To Bob) The blue-gray rock, on closer examination, looks like a partially re-crystallized fragment breccia.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Are you going to get the "afters" in there?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I want to do a little bit better documentation on this thing.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I'm going to go over and look at that contact.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: ...I got a few close-up stereos of the inclusion that we tried to sample, and I'm going to see if I can't give you a little flightline stereo around this thing.
If I can stay on my feet.
(No answer; long pause) Do you read me, Jack, okay?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And Houston reads you loud and clear, also.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (To Bob) You can see where we've been pounding on this rock.
We didn't succeed in getting samples everywhere.
(Pause) And I'm giving you a "90-degree corner".
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Bob, it looks to me like there are inclusions of blue-gray in the gabbro...(correcting himself) in the anorthositic gabbro.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Are you saying you think this whole big...You think this whole big blue-gray thing is an inclusion?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And there's some little ones over here.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Yeah, but then within the blue-gray, we've got all these other fragments.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: It's just several generations of activity; and it looks like the gabbro, though, picked up the fragmental breccia as inclusions.
(Pause) Bob, It really looks that way right now.
There's a small one here in the...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, Charlie (Duke) is here mumbling something about it looking just like House Rock.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: I'll tell you, it's not a breccia, not like House Rock.
Not to take anything away from House Rock, though.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Hey, Bob, there's a lot of mantling on a very shallow slope of a fracture here on one of the upslope blocks (Fragment 1).
I would assume it's just part of the talus picked up as it's rolled down.
But if it's worth sampling, you might think about it.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, Gene, if you can get that fairly readily, why don't you...You can perhaps just scoop it up with the bag.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: That's exactly what I can do.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: If you can get up to the rock there.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: It will be in my flight line stereo, and it's going to be bag 557.
And I'll take an "after" and show you where it came from.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: This is the easiest part of the rock in the world to work.
Man, here's a big white clast.
There's one on top about a foot and a half across, and here's one.
(Pause) Feel like a kid playing in a sandbox.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Balancing on his downhill leg) Well, Bob, I think I've done the best I can.
I'd say that they're pretty clearly inclusions of blue-gray in the anorthositic gabbro here near the contact.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And Gene, your bag is hanging by one hook there.
Be careful, if you can...(Correcting himself) Or, LMP...
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Okay, I'll have Jack fix my bag.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, Bob, by accident...I didn't think I could do it, but I got a sample of the inclusion.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Hey, Jack, that's your bag (SCB) that's hanging by one hook.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Oh, they're talking to me, huh?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I didn't think they could see me.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And it's blue-gray with light colored...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Presenting his SCB) Put these in my bag.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (To Bob) But the whole thing (Fragment 2) seems to be pretty well altered or metamorphosed...compared to the major rock (Fragment 1) we just sampled...(compared) to the other blue-gray rock.
(Pause) This bag (Gene's SCB cover) is terrible.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Man, there's a dark hole in there where you...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Here's another bag to put in there before you go away.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Taking a full sample bag from Gene) Oh, okay.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Just put the thumb on one side, and I'll...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Now let me fix your bag.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, Bob, I think that inclusion will give you an example of what...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: ...what the anorthositic gabbro did to the blue-gray breccia.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And we're ready for you guys to leave this rock and press on and either get the rake soil and cores near that crater down below the rock just a shade (that is, a short distance), or else go on to some other different variety rocks in the area.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Well, I tell you, going down to that crater is not a problem.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, well, find a decent area to get the rake soil from and a couple of cores.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Tell you what, Gene, I could go down there and start a rake, and you could come down there.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Yeah, I don't think you ought to try and walk back up, Jack.
Let me get a pan from right here where I got this sample; (that is, upslope of Fragment 1).
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I'm going to come over and...I'll go get the rake and get the gnomon.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: 17, it's not that vital to get to that crater.
We just need a good place for a rake soil and a double...(correcting himself) a single core.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (Standing in the split, examining Fragment 2 and not facing Gene) Hey, get uphill a little bit, if you can, for the pan, so that you don't...so you see my other pan station.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: It was over there in that crater, just uphill from the Rover.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Hey, and, 17, we aren't all that gung-ho about that particular crater, if it's that much of a job to get down to it and back up.
We just need a decent place for a rake soil sample and a single core.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Bob, we don't move around from here too much.
I tell you, these slopes are something else.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: We agree with that, from what we see on the television.
So use your judgment, and get them where it's the best place
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Well, you might take a look at me walking up.
But I don't think I can get to the top.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I just got to get a place I can get a pan from.
(Pause) (Gesturing) Okay, now I left the gnomon down there.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I'll have to go get it.
(Pause) I think we'll setup right here near the Rover.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I think I'll go and save some water, back on Intermediate.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Pause) Bob, from up here, the light mantle is not evident until you see the angular reflection up on the scarp.
Very thin, light patches might be evident out on the valley, but not nearly as pronounced as I might have thought from this altitude.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Pause) You know, Jack, when we finish with Station 8, we will have covered this whole valley from corner to corner!
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Yeah, but I didn't think we'd ever really quite get to that far corner.
And we're going to make it!
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Having taken another opportunity to examine the boulders) Bob, that blue-gray rock near the contact with the anorthositic gabbro does get some vesicles in it.
I think they'll show up in Gene's pictures.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I just ran out of film at 160.
And I'm about two pictures short of the pan, and they (the missing frames)'re upslope.
I think I can cover most of that with the 500.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Are) you going to go to the Rover and change your mag now?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Well, Jack's going to need some help from me.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Let me know when you get to the Rover to change the mags after you get done with that, and I'll tell you what mag to change.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: But press on and help Jack with those first.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Jack, if you got enough film, I'll just come and help you.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Pause) (Calling to Bob) Remind me to dust my camera, too, will you?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Don't forget to dust your camera.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: We'll keep track of that for you, Gene.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Man, I tell you, these slopes are great.
I wouldn't mind being up on top coming down, but...Hey, that boulder track is quite a trench!
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: That thing must be a meter or 2 deep, huh!?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Wouldn't it be easier to (pull the) rake downhill.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: It would, but the stuff wouldn't stay in.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Pause) Make sure you get that one by the...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Long Pause) We're not really supposed to be selective about raking.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: No you're not (being selective), you're just covering the area.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: That's why I set up there.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: A selective sample is better than no sample at all.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Let me put some in there (meaning, in the bag Gene is holding).
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Let me go another couple of swipes.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: There's one(rock) a couple of inches (across).
Most of them are an inch or so or smaller.
Some of them look like the inclusions.
As a matter of fact, the ones that are broken open look like some of the light-colored inclusions we saw in the big boulder.
The others are too dust covered to say anything about.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: A couple of them look fairly coarsely crystalline.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Laughs) We ended up with three more.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Let me get an "after," such as it is.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: You want to put that in (Jack's SCB)?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Yeah, I'd better put it in before I...(Pause) Okay.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And, 17, our present plans from the (Backroom) are (that) we'd like to get the single core, the 500 millimeter shots - and, I guess, maybe one could do one, and one could do the other.
And then we'd like to press on and do a short Station 7, unless you think you have got a fair variety of rocks here.
The feeling is that you have (garbled) significant variety of rocks.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Little more, little more, little more.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I'll get the core and let Jack get the 500's.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I think we've pretty much covered the general variety we've seen here.
I think we're seeing most of them in that boulder.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And so we'd like to go on to Station 7 then - when you get the 500 and the core - in hopes of finding a variation of boulders along the front.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (To Jack) Let me know when you get it.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, why don't you get the 500, and I'll get the core.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And, Gene, if you want to change, we recommended magazine Foxtrot or Fran (Magazine 146), as the case may be.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (To Jack) Don't forget to get (a 500 photo of) that boulder track.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Talking through Jack's following remark) And while you're at it, Gene, ...
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: This once, I'm going to have to lean against the Rover to do it.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: ...you might remember to dust your camera when you're leaning over the seat.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Let me look at your camera.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: If this Rover wasn't here, we'd roll downhill.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I think we could use an "upper" (drive tube) here if you want to save the lowers.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Why don't you get your 500, and I'll...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: But do you want a core?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: The core's in there, isn't it?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Well, there's some under my seat if you want to use those.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: We have three lowers and two uppers, so we'd just as soon use the extra lower here in the single core.
That'll give us two uppers and two lowers left...
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: There should be a lower in there, Geno.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Bob, any special place you want that?
Just out here on the slope?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Should have put the gnomon up.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Just out there on the slope.
(Pause) (Talking through Jack's following remark) I guess if you saw a crater you might look at that,...
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (To Gene) You don't have any film to document, either.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: ...but primarily we're looking at the crater.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (Pause) We have a couple of dents in our wheels.
That's better than having a flat tire.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (To Jack) Did he say "in a crater"?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I'm not sure what he said.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: How do I get this doggone...(Garbled) turn to come off?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: How am I going to see up there to shoot this thing?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Well, why don't you lean against the rock?
Go over there and lean against it.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Well, I have to do something.
I was trying to get so I could lean against the Rover, but that ain't going to work.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: The Rover isn't steady enough for you to lean against.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: There's just no place to lean.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And, Jack, and if you'll listen for a minute, I'll tell you some possible 500-millimeter targets the people have in mind.
One, the LM, if you can see it from there.
Two, Nansen, if you can see it from there.
In other words, I guess they're talking about looking along your traverse from yesterday.
It would be mostly the back shots, apparently.
And then, also, the South Massif, and I don't know what you can get of boulder tracks leading up the North Massif.
And most of those will be looking downhill towards the LM, Stations 2, 3, and 4.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Yeah, the LM is visible by the way.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I got a set of (pause) what looks like the outcrop from which the boulder came.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I'm afraid they're moved a little bit.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I got a few pictures looking up the boulder track and then off to the left a little bit; and one off to the right.
And I think...I'm not sure how well they overlap; that's just an awful hard shot.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Pause) And if you're done with that, have you got a frame count - or, you're still taking them, I guess, it looks like.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Magazine Foxtrot is on about frame 2, and I cycled through it.
And I've got the core all set, and I'm going to go get it.
And I didn't hear where you said to put it, Bob.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And did you get your camera...
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Not the bottom of a small crater, huh?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And did you get your camera dusted?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: I got it all dusted and the mag's changed.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I'll even get you a picture of it.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Can you get the LM from there?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: That core went in very easy, Bob.
I pushed it in about a quarter of the way.
And about another five or six whacks, and it's in all the way.
(Long Pause as he takes two pictures of the core tube) Okay.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Shorty, and Station 3, and Station 2, and what else?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And any sort of outcrop you see in the South Massif.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: No, I mean the other day.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Stereo is stereo is stereo, I guess.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Okay, and I got you a little soil mech(anics)...(Stops to listen to Bob)
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (To Bob) Well, but it's not stereo; it's right along the same line.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: ...little soil mechanics of the hole, which stayed intact; very nice and round.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Reaching the gate) Yes, sir, we got a couple of dented tires!
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: You aren't going to get anything else out of me if I keep taking pictures.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: A dented tire is a little, oh, a little golf-ball size or smaller indentation in the mesh.
How does that sound to you?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: That sounds like a dented tire; that's how it sounds.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: That's sort of like what it is.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I guess we can get to the next station with that.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Yeah, I got a brand new mag on.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And we'd like to get you guys rolling as soon as feasible there.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: I'll need your rammer, so if you'll just turn right.
(Making a seismic-charge joke) Pin's out; core tube is safe.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: You take this and put this under your seat, if you want, Jack.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Going to his seat, carrying the core tube) Don't lose that (scoop).
(Garbled, but probably meaning "I wouldn't know") what to do (without it).
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Did you give them the (core tube) number?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Copy that; under the LMP's seat.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: No, I was just sneaking up, that's all.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (I'll) come and let you lean on me.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I wish we...The one thing I didn't do.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Going around the front of the Rover) Didn't get pictures of those foliated vesicles.
I don't think the ones you had were in that kind of rock.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I don't want to lose that thing (the
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, 17, when you get back on here, we don't need any charges, and we'll leave the SEP turned off.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: I'm not sure...Did you turn it off, Gene?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: We want to move on to (Station) 7 here.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Open the gate, and I'll bring one.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And let me know when you get ready to get back on the Rover there, 17.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (To Jack) Guess what isn't opening again.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: You could put them under Jack's seat if it's easier.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: What do you want done to the SEP while I'm here?
Do you want the blanket left open?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: We'd like the blankets closed and taped down again, if possible, and both switches left off.
We won't touch it again until Station 8.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Oh, the tape's not going to stick anymore, I'm afraid.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I need a normal sample bag for one here.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Pause) Oh, that doggoned thing's not going to...That tape is full of dust now.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Here, let me get this big one (meaning 76055).
I'm about ready to drop it.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And 560 has an undocumented, except by the pans, very white...(It) looks like a crushed anorthosite.
It looks like some of the inclusions in the breccia...(correcting himself) gray, re-crystallized breccia.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Jack, when you get around, and we close this gate, you might try and hit that top of that SEP down again.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Hey, Bob, you're staying keyed an awful long time.
We can hear a lot of what's going on back there.
Let me get this out of the way.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Well, because it (the gate)'s not locked.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And, before you get on, remember to close the battery covers if they...
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Your camera lens looks all right, Geno.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Cover closed...(Going to the SEP receiver behind his seat) Okay.
Do they want it on or off?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Leave it off, but try and close that cover as best you can.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Well, I'm afraid the tape has had it.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Leaning across his seat) You want us to tape it again, Bob?
(To Gene) What did you do with the tape?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: If you can grab the tape right off, but don't spend a lot of time on it.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (To Gene) What did you do with that tape?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Let's worry about it at Station 7, if we're going to worry about it.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: It's too hard to work on here, and it's not going to take just a minute.
It's going to take too much time.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I'm not sure I can get back on here.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Well, let me give you a hand.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: We need any a...We don't need any...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Why don't you drive down and get so you're not...You can get on...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: You can go downhill very easy.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Let me get the TV; The battery covers are closed.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Why don't you just go down there.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: ...I'll carry the Rover sampler, just in case.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I'll get that out of your way, too.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I'll head down to that...Actually, I'll sidehill over to those boulders right over there and then see if that's any change (in rock type).
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: You might, if you get another sample, a large sample, you might grab it, and we'll throw it in the footpan here.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And I'll see if I can't find a level spot to...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I sort of ought to have my scoop, too.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: No, don't take too much; just take that (LRV Sampler).
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: How about letting me have your hammer, then?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay; and, 17, can you verify that the gnomon is back in the Rover?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: The rake is on the Rover.
We got the...You put the core under your (seat) pan, right?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I'm going to power up and see if I can't come down and get you.
Boy, that boulder track is impressive.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay; and, 17, when you get moving...
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: ...we want to get, and I quote, "a maximum variety of hand samples with a minimum amount of documentation, in a minimum amount of time at Station 7".
It's just an attempt to see what kind of variety we can get along the face of the front.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Well, I'm not sure I can get on without ending up in your seat.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Static clears) I probably ought to turn my water off of Max if that's where it is.
I don't want to run out today.
(Long Pause) Well, the roll indicator says 15 degrees; and the pitch indicator says about 12 (giving a total slope of nearly 20 degrees).
I don't know if I believe all that.
transcript.speaker: Cernan
